input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download)
- Install the **latest .NET 8 SDK** (or .NET 9 if you want the newest). MAUI ships as a workload inside the SDK.
- Verify: `dotnet --version`

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- Discover available workloads: `dotnet workload search`
- Verify: `dotnet workload list` — should show `maui` as installed.

## 3. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [https://developer.apple.com/xcode/](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- After install, accept the license and install CLI tools:

```bash
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
xcode-select --install
```

- Verify: `xcodebuild -version`
- Open Xcode → **Settings → Platforms** → download the iOS simulator runtime you need.

## 4. Install Android Tooling

You have two options:

**Option A — Visual Studio Code + .NET MAUI extension (lightweight):**
- Install [VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/) + the **.NET MAUI** extension.
- The extension will prompt you to install missing Android SDK components automatically.

**Option B — Manual via Android command-line tools:**
- **Source:** [https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only](https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only)
- Download the macOS command-line tools, then:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/Library/Android/sdk/cmdline-tools
# unzip into ~/Library/Android/sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/

sdkmanager "platform-tools" "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0" \
           "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a" "emulator"
```

- Set environment variables in `~/.zshrc`:

```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin:$PATH"
```

- Accept licenses: `sdkmanager --licenses`

## 5. Install JDK (Android requirement)

- .NET MAUI bundles a compatible JDK via the workload, but if needed:
- **Source:** [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation) (lists the required JDK version)
- Microsoft OpenJDK: [https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)

## 6. Discover the Right Versions

| Component | How to check required version |
|---|---|
| .NET SDK | `dotnet --version` / release notes at github.com/dotnet/maui |
| MAUI workload | `dotnet workload search maui` |
| Xcode | Apple's release notes; MAUI docs list minimum Xcode version |
| Android SDK | `dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies` auto-resolves |
| JDK | MAUI docs specify (currently JDK 17) |

## 7. Verify Everything End-to-End

```bash
# Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

If all four commands succeed, your environment is fully configured.

## 8. Official Reference

The single authoritative guide:
[https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation)
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a setup guide for .NET MAUI development on macOS targeting iOS and Android. This is a knowledge/advice question — they explicitly say they can't run commands and want a plan. No tools needed, just a well-structured answer.
